reviews.title: No Bottle mineral water in the room
reviews.text:
Upon inquiring about a bottle of in-house mineral water for my suite room at 2523, I was informed that it would incur an additional charge at RM25+ per bottle, which I found quite surprising given the expectations associated with a 5-star establishment like the Ritz-Carlton. What followed was even more concerning: when I called guest services to request a bottle, the staff abruptly hung up on me. As a result, I was forced to go down to the front desk in person to make the request.
This is far from the standard of service I expected, and it has certainly tarnished my experience here. I wanted to bring this to your attention as I believe it does not align with the reputation and service excellence that the Ritz-Carlton is known for.
Lastly, why hotel guest parking is chargeable? Compared to Traders shangrila KUL,. Westin , Grand Milennum will just wave off my parking. 

Will not consider this hotel in the future .

reviews.title: Needs much updating and staff training
reviews.text:
We booked a 2 bedroom suite for 5 days. It's a spacious suite with ample toilets (4 to be precise) which is a plus , a decent sized kitchen with dishwasher and a lovely little study room! However the suite did not come with a washing machine, which would have been useful.  

But the issues we faced were with the poor / under-trained staff. The staff at the swimming pool are extremely under-trained and completely unaware of the menu and could not explain anything about the dishes.  Food and drinks took very long to be  serviced and it was the same struggle with in-room dining orders. 

The complimentary coffee and tea offered in the room are not at par with the quality and standing of a Ritz Carlton brand. There is no bottled water. Only a big water filter installation in the kitchen. The hotel and it's facilities feel very dated. We also noticed moss on the side of the building where the pool is situated. 

The hotel amenities and service did not live  up to its brand name in our opinion.

Only overly cheap fried food is left for guests and some salad bar would still be on. Because I like to breakfast at 9-9.30am, I would have to ask them go backstage and find me some salmon since that is what I eat and that is what should’ve been expected and served to all guests anyway, not just doing it for show. They would make me wait standing there for 10 minutes each day just to annoy me and then would serve me some salmon and good cheese. 

I would not go back to Ritz anymore until Admin changes this gang of India dudes that make guests stay uncomfortable. However, if you are okay to eat fried spicy curry and cheap white rice and stuff, you will be fine.

reviews.title: RUDE STAFF ROOM MANAGER
reviews.text:
Very Disappointing Room Service Experience

Dear Ritz carlton Residence Management,

I am writing to express my disappointment regarding the service I received during my recent stay 5 oct - 8 oct 2025. I stayed ritz carlton residence with room type suite with 2 bedroom. 
3 adults & 2 children in 1 Room. 
When I called the room services by phone at 6 Oct 2025 at 11 PM
 to request refill a face cotton( they didn't refill it in my bathroom )
 the duty room manager sent a person to deliver the things.  Unfortunately, when the staff come into my room. The door was opened by my kids babysitter & she didnt know that I request something. when she open the door while I was in the toilet. 
So I call again the room service by phone and she was very Rude. She Mad & shouting at me , "why you said you didnt order anything when my friend come to your room?!! " 
I tried to tell her that in our room have 5 person & the person who open the dior when the staff come, didnt know I was request something from the room services. Please deliver it again , I need it. 
 her response was unexpectedly rude. She told me, “No! Your room cannot request anything from now.”
When I politely asked for her name, she replied, “I don’t have a name!! ” and abruptly ended the call while shouting at me.

This experience was extremely unpleasant and far below the standard I expected from your hotel. I hope the management will look into this matter seriously and ensure that better service and professionalism are maintained in the future.

Note
Stay from 5 oct - 8 oct 2025
Type of room: Suite 2 bedroom

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
I am writing to express my anger and disappointment regarding my stay at the Ritz-Carlton Kuala Lumpur on April 1st, 2025.

I arrived at the lobby at 1:30 PM, then i was told that I could not check in because my room was not ready，which i am completely understand towards that situation as i am arriving early.

After waiting for an hour, I was informed that the room was in housekeeping. An hour later, another staff member told me the delay was due to the previous guests checking out late，and they “promise” me that will let me check in before 5pm. 

I don't understand why i need to wait until 5pm，while ur website show that the check in time is 3:00pm, and none of these reasons are my responsibility. When my friend arrived， they are very angry and ask the counter only then I get my room.

I had three luggage racks full of belongings, which I know took up space, but I couldn’t do anything about it since my room wasn’t ready. This clearly shows poor coordination between the front desk and housekeeping.

While I waited, I was offered complimentary coffee or tea at the lobby lounge, but that only felt like a way to buy time rather than provide any real help. 

I had planned a photography session for my birthday celebration in my booked room on 3:15. The delay forced me to extend the session with the photographer, resulting in extra charges for 1 hours charges. In the end, I had to pay double of what we originally agreed upon， around RM 1000. 

Additionally, I had a dinner reservation at 5:30 PM. Because of the delays, I had to rush through dinner, but can only arrive 2 hours late because of my photography session in the room had been delayed, and hence I need to pay extra as my reservation is cancel and  need to pay the rate to enter into private room.

I was very disappointed with my stay. I do not expect this kind of service from a 5-star hotel in the center of Kuala Lumpur. I booked a 3-bedroom suite and expected top-notch service, but instead, my birthday celebration was completely ruined.

reviews.title: The Not SO Ritzy Ritz Carlton
reviews.text:
Stayed at the Ritz Carlton KL on the last few days of our Week long KL trip. I'm comparing my experiences at this hotel in comparison to the Sheraton PJ which we stayed for 5 nights at prior to checking in here. 
Off the Cuff, the Ritz has all the Plushness and Pazazz as expected. However I'll breakdown my experience to Pros and Cons below :

Pros
1. Location - easily can access Malls, restaurants and the famous Bar and Food streets in Jalan Alor and Bukit Bintang

2. Spacious Rooms

Cons 

1. The bed was very hard and extremely uncomfortable. The Sheraton Mattress was heavenly compared to this

2. Coffee machine in the Room was malfunctioning, was replaced by another which was also malfunctioning :/ the coffee provided in the room was also very ordinary

3. Ordered breakfast to the Room (Eggs Benny with my American Breakfast Option) - was served Orange Cordial, when I requested was Fresh Juice I was informed that it is chargeable and not included in the American Breakfast (Approx. RM 90) - even a 3 Star Hotel provides Fresh Juice (forget about the Oranges, at least the seasonally available fruit)

4. Decided to stay in and order room service and watch movies on one of the evenings, at 8.00pm when I called room service to order a Cocktail for my wife, I was told that non were available and that the Bar was CLOSED !!!!! 

5. The concierge was not helpful at all to collect our Uber Eats order :/


I would NOT ever consider the KL Ritz ever again.

reviews.title: Do not stay for now!
reviews.text:
I stayed here before and it was a really pleasant one so we decided to come back but we regretted it BIG TIME!
No one informed us that the hotel is going thru a renovation when we booked. Even upon arriving, we drove to the carpark but was told that it was closed and the nearest parking will be at the mall about 5min walk away or can choose for valet at RM30 and its not even sheltered! We didnt want to walk all the way to the mall so we had no other choice but to valet it. Went out 4 times in 1 day and had to pay rm30 every time we drive out. Its ridiculous as we are staying in the hotel and not given any complimentary parking???
Upon checking in,NO ONE told us the even the rooms in higher floors are undergoing renovation too. Was woken up at 9am by the loud drilling. Called FO and was told that the works will only end in the evening.
The room we got was small, very old and tired.. So different compared to our first stay.
Should we have known that the hotel is revamping, we wouldnt stay here. Was so thankful that we only booked for a night. And was relieved to check out the next day and move to another hotel
DO NOT STAY THERE TILL THE RENO IS COMPLETE. Major nightmare!

reviews.title: The Ritz Carlton starter pack
reviews.text:
Before I start, I will definitely highlight the big pro of this hotel which is the location, it is a definite winner. But………… everything else was a shock to the system if one were to compare to other Ritz Carlton’s in the world. Check in was decent after a late night flight but when we got into the room, phone was spoilt, had to head down all the way to request for technician and for room needs. Needed a hot shower to end off the night but it drizzled out like warm water. Surely ritz kl can do better with their pillows at such a rate. Room was awfully quiet in the nights which amplified the surrounding sounds (be it from the outside or just within the building) were awfully loud. And pls do get the carpets cleaned thoroughly alongside the obvious stains on the sofas. 

 Breakfast was super underwhelming and not impressive, with drink requests forgotten, food queues long, juices wiped out. 

All in all, this version of ritz would be a sorely dated one that needs a revamp somehow but then as mentioned it is indeed a starter pack to ritz Carlton at an entry priced rate although there are many better hotels in KL going at that very same rate. But will emphasize again, location has been a winner.

reviews.title: Trip
reviews.text:
We booked 3 rooms at the beginning and end of our trip. 
The suite booked was excellent and large. Incredible value for money! 
The service was not great. One night I ordered food that took 2 hours to come. The front desk staff, were not very professional. We missed our first night due to a flight delay. We emailed and let the front desk know. The second night when we arrived our rooms were given away. The front desk were all shouting at each other. 
The pool was out of use. 
Second leg of the trip, we thought we would get an upgrade due to the mess up in the first part but none given. Strange ac noises in the room that had to have maintenance fix. Shower temperature was temperamental. 
Breakfast was great however for a ritz Carlton very few restaurant options. The in room dining menu is limited. 
Overall I felt for a ritz Carlton, I expected better standard and service.

reviews.title: My disappointing stay at the Ritz
reviews.text:
I was staying in the 2 bedroom suite and was totally disappointed with the service I got from housekeeping.
1) Both the wardrobes had electrical wires dangerously dangling out of them and as far as I can tell it was put in place by masking tape that has since worn off. The clothes hangers that were given were in bad condition and dirty
2) My toiletries were not refilled I had to ask for more when I needed extra. Even the cheap bedroom slippers were rationed.
3) My small bottles of mineral water were only replenished on the 1st night and no more after.
4) I had no minibar and snacks in my suite
5) My room TV wasn't working and the remote was missing. In the end I found out that the TV had no power cable connected it!
6) The suite had no basic proper stationary except for a pen.
7) Last but not least the weighting scale in the toilet had a flat battery. Why bother to put it in there when it doesn't work?
I had the duty manager come up and went thru this long list with her and she wasn't even sure what is suppose to be in the suites and was certainly surprised at the standard of the room and housekeeping.

I am sorry but I honestly expected the Ritz to do much better.In the end I ask myself is this really the highly rated Ritz Carlton Hotel that I am staying in? The standard of the room and housekeeping is nowhere close to other hotels who provide much better service and amenities not forgetting housekeeping! The total experience was just a total disappointment. I am definitely not going back to stay there again.

reviews.title: Its the Ritz... 'nuff said
reviews.text:
its a great establishment and ritz won't be known as ritz if it hadn't live up to a certain standard. if you want to stay here, you obviously know what you want from your hotel stay because they don't come cheap.

the room we got in ritz kl was very large, spacious and almost totally decorated in gold, white and royal blue. the bed was huge and pretty awesome! we went for the exec deluxe so the room itself was a lot larger than the deluxe ones - separate area with sofa for watching tv, dvd player, toilet is huge (you can practically dance in there!) and very clean.

the only two problem i had with my stay was:

1) hotel is located quite a distance away from civilisation and bukit bintang. its the quieter end of bukit bintang and you'll have to walk a good 10 mins from the hotel, pass through jw marriott (linked to ritz via a skybridge) before you can get to the street. i guess you can walk outside, but in a worse case scenario, like when it rains - then you'll have no choice. so location is not ideal.

the other is staff. banquet staffs are superb - very attentive, very friendly. but front office staff was sub-par, at least for my check-in. it was quite disappointing. service was very slow, and the guys who checked us in were not as friendly and accomodating as i expected them to be. they represented ritz carlton - and it was disappointing.

but good stay nonetheless. worth the money paid for.
reviews.positives: Big rooms, great bed - almost immaculate

reviews.title: Review: The Ritz-Carlton, Kuala Lumpur Stayed: 16th – 19th | Floor: 18th
reviews.text:
My recent stay at The Ritz-Carlton, Kuala Lumpur was a pleasant and relaxing experience, with a few minor inconveniences along the way.  

Check-In & Room Upgrade
Prior to arrival, I had contacted the Concierge regarding an early check-in request (12-1 PM). While the response was courteous, check-in remained subject to room availability. Upon arriving at approximately 2 PM, the room was unfortunately not ready, and I had to wait until 3 PM. However, the hotel compensated by upgrading my stay from a Deluxe Room King to an Executive Deluxe King, which was a delightful surprise. Additionally, when requesting a later check-out (until 2 or 3 PM), the hotel kindly offered a complimentary one-hour extension from 12 PM to 1 PM.  

Room & Comfort
The room itself was spacious, offering a comfortable ambiance. However, there were noticeable dust accumulations in the corners of the room. While the bed was generally fine, movements from another person could be felt, slightly affecting sleep quality. The pillow was not as plush as expected, which slightly impacted overall comfort.  

A notable issue was encountered on the second day when the sink became choked, but this was promptly rectified after informing room service. The service team deserves appreciation for their attentiveness, especially for taking the extra initiative to convert the side sofa into a bed for my child, adding a thoughtful touch to the stay.  

Hotel Location & Amenities
Staying on the 18th floor, I found the hotel to be quiet and peaceful, situated away from the bustling streets, making it a relaxing retreat. Despite its serene ambiance, it remained highly accessible, with a link bridge via Starhill offering a convenient walk to Pavilion Shopping Centre, allowing easy access to shopping and dining options.  

The swimming pool area is relatively small with limited views, but for those who value privacy, it offers a secluded and tranquil space to unwind.  

Service & Atmosphere
One of the hotel’s strongest points was its exceptionally warm and welcoming staff. Every interaction was met with a friendly greeting, creating a refined yet inviting atmosphere. A particularly thoughtful touch by the hotel was the nightly service, where pieces of complimentary cake were delivered along with an inspirational quote from a famous personality, adding a unique and charming element to the stay.  

Overall, The Ritz-Carlton, Kuala Lumpur provided a quiet and enjoyable stay. Despite minor setbacks, such as the initial room availability, some maintenance concerns, and pillow firmness, the attentive service, serene environment, and thoughtful gestures made it a luxurious yet relaxing getaway.

reviews.title: Value for money but not a luxury experience
reviews.text:
The great thing was the size of the suite we had. This made the overall experience enjoyable. However in comparison to the Ritz Carlton in other countries there were multiple areas which need to be fixed (not just in the sense of being addressed but literally fixed). 

Handles on closet doors either came off or were close to coming off. Edges of the sofa were worn down and ragged. The same goes for the cushions. 
Despite the suite being huge we struggled to find any bins. I'm the end there was one under the the two dressing counters. 

The pool was disappointing. There are two pools but the only one available to kids under 16 was deeper than the other. The gym`s facilities were average at best with dumbbells maxed at 20kgs. 

The coat performance was good but I hope the hotel will fix the problems and understand it is sometimes these very small things which can make a significant difference.

reviews.title: Fantastic location but a very dated hotel that needs refreshing
reviews.text:
What a great location, we were of walking distance to great shopping and food locations.
The lobby staff were fantastic, shout out to the concierge manager who helped me a lot with my mobile phone and printing my script for emcee wedding for my dear friends' wedding. What a great gym and spa facilities - great to work out, then sauna steam and jacuzzi after.
Unfortunately everything else like the swimming pool and our two bedroom suite were very dated and needs a whole lot of refreshing. I was also expecting more utensils for a full sized kitchen but we had empty cupboards for the most part!
Also I dont know why but our daily housekeeping only arrived very late during the day like 4pm or 5pm when we needed to use the room to change for dinner or to attend a wedding  - why not earlier between 12-2pm when we stepped out for lunch or to get our hair and makeup done. So I had a guy changing our bedsheets whilst I was rushing to get into my wedding guest outfit! There were rusts around windows panels , I hope they consider renovations soon!

reviews.title: A Truly Grand Experience...
reviews.text:
My recent stay at The Ritz-Carlton, Kuala Lumpur was nothing short of perfect. From the moment we stepped into the hotel, we were welcomed by the sheer elegance of its interiors—sophisticated, luxurious, and immaculately maintained. The grandeur of the lobby alone set the tone for a remarkable stay.

The service throughout our visit was exceptional. A special mention goes to the breakfast experience at The Cobalt Restaurant, which was a daily highlight. The variety and quality of food were outstanding, but what truly elevated the experience was the warm and attentive staff—Tarikul and Nilufa in particular, whose service was gracious and always with a smile.

The security staff, especially Jahid, deserves recognition for being not only professional but also incredibly warm and helpful. His cheerful demeanor and guidance made us feel welcomed and safe at all times.

Late one evening, we faced a minor internet issue in our room, and I was impressed by the night staff on duty who responded promptly, resolved the problem with care, and even brought in drinks to make us feel at ease. Their dedication to guest comfort was truly commendable.

The front desk team was consistently friendly, professional, and proactive. They made check-in and check-out seamless, offered to take lovely photos of us in the stunning grand lobby, and ensured we felt at home throughout our stay.

If there's one area of improvement, it would be the internet connection in the room. As I had some work to do, the occasional connectivity issues were a bit frustrating. I hope this is something the hotel will enhance moving forward.

That said, the overall experience was outstanding in every other aspect. Beautiful surroundings, incredible service, and a genuine sense of hospitality make The Ritz-Carlton, Kuala Lumpur a top-tier destination. I would return in a heartbeat.

Rating: 5/5 – A perfect stay.

reviews.title: Classic stay
reviews.text: I had a 2 night stay at the Ritz - basically for business.  The location of the hotel is excellent, very close by all amenities that one would need.  Close to a shopping malls, restaurants, etc.  The only donwside would be the parking facility.  One can easily miss the entrance to its parking area at the basement.  The staff were also not properly informed about whether parking fee would be imposed for guests.  Other than this it was a pleasant stay.

reviews.title: Ritz Carlton KL
reviews.text: Staff was very professional and helpful. We were upgraded to give us a non-smoker room. It was Friday night and even though we were on the 16th floor there was a deep bass pounding from a nearby music club that made it difficult to fall asleep. We had an early morning flight the next day so we were not well rested and the a/c vent was dirty and the air quality was not good.  We told the hotel manager.  He compensated us by paying for our taxi ride to airport. Overall a bit disappointed for Ritz hotel experience.

reviews.title: Excellent Pool & Service
reviews.text:
Location is in a good area - within a few hundred meters of the best malls and restaurants and within walking distance of the Petronas Towers.  Service was excellent.  Michelle at the front desk attended to every need and made several great suggestions for places to visit, restaurant, where to get a haircut etc. 

Pool was laid out well.  Although not much of a view or scenery from the surroundings it had the feel of a garden in the middle of a major city.  They did an excellent job here.  The gym was small but adequate for a light workout (maximum 50 lbs dumb bells which is normal), however, not much free weights.  The area isn't conducive to running outside so had to resort to the treadmills (Lifecycle). 

Some minor grievances are the lack of some little touches such as the area under the bathroom sink being bare concrete with no covering.  Also our guest had to pay a fee for parking their car at the hotel which I was informed was not standard for high end hotels in the area.

reviews.text: it is one of the less impressive hotels in the chain. the lobby was so small as to be practically non-existent. also it was dark. in contrast, the adjoining ritz-carlton residence has a spacious and well-lit lobby, which i found very strange indeed, as though the people living in the hotel are inferior to those in the residence. the hotel identity is diluted because it shares facilities with the residence. the gourmet cafe is charming, with attentive staff, but the bakery was pretty awful for a place at the top of the hotel classification. it's location is great, for it allowed me access to a wide range of restaurants and cafes more diverse and interesting than the ones of the hotel. i was also plagued by a disfunctional toilet for two days which the hotel people could not reapir. for the third night, i was finally moved, after two days of inconvenience, to a suite at the same price. but i didn't enjoy the large space as i was alone and had to leave very early the following morning anyway.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: Gorgeous, quiet, comfortable room. Big spacious bathroom (with a scale 😊). Awesome water pressure in shower. Superb service.
reviews.negatives: You know how a hotel can come so close to perfect, but then do a single thing really short-sighted that negates all the hard work by the earnest staff? This hotel makes you sign out its umbrellas on a clipboard and makes a big fuss of pointing out that you'll be charged a punitive $25 (for a $3 umbrella) if you don't return it. I felt so much stress from the threat (and possibility of losing it somewhere) that I didn't dare "rent" the umbrella and got wet searching for my own to buy in a convenience store (yes, for $3). I have stayed at nice hotels all over the world, and every one of them lends umbrellas free of charge and relies on their guests to be honest about it. This won't be enough to prevent me from returning, but judging from the back room full of unused umbrellas that I saw, I'm not the only guest not offering up his name for the clipboard.

reviews.title: Ritz Carlton Kuala Lumpur, one of the top 5 Hotels in Kuala Lumpur
reviews.positives:
Hello it is not our first time being in Kuala Lumpur and we have stayed in multiple different hotels here. 

I really like Ritz Carlton Kuala Lumpur as a whole, the rooms are great, staffs are great as well (particularly the sleek Indian gentlemen that is stationed near the bridge to Starhill who greets everyone good night and sweet dreams).

I went for my traditional Malay massage at Spa Village which was really extraordinary, to a point that I slept. Kudos to the Indonesian female masseur (sorry I forgot her name) but she was really really skilled!
reviews.negatives:
Rest assured that the following that I am commenting is partly due to the urban planning of Bukit Bintang and nothing particular to the staffs of Ritz Carlton. 

But yes there are certain facilities which I did not use because of the surrounding environment, particularly the pool and gym which seems to be really enclosed (I think for future expansion reference, swimming pool should be located at the top of the building).

The front entrance road of the hotel seems to be jammed most of the time which is really a hassle for us to keep waiting for our grab for about 10-20 minutes. (but then again the hotel is located within a busy traffic).

reviews.title: The Ritz-Carlton for the first time in my life.
reviews.text: In November 2024, based on the recommendation of a friend who lived in KL for ten years on an overseas assignment, I stayed at the Ritz in KL for three nights at a reasonable rate. The check-in was in English, but the service was courteous, and I was advised to take advantage of the club lounge, which allowed me to enjoy breakfast, lunch, tea time, a light dinner, and dessert at night all in one day. The highlight was the breakfast buffet. My family goes out more than 12 times a year, but this was the best breakfast buffet I've had recently. There is a walkway to the Bukit Bintang area, connecting it to the Marriott, and right in front of the exit is the Pavilion. Additionally, I was pleased that my requests were met. The only disappointment was that there was no cleaning on the first day, and I complained about it. After the cleaning, a set of apology chocolates was left, which made me happy.

reviews.title: Stunning Hotel in the heart of KL
reviews.text: We booked a two bedroomed Suite at the Ritz just after Christmas. It is a beautifully designed hotel in the heart of the Golden Triangle in KL.  Overall had a lovely experience, staff were very attentive and the service was exceptional. Only disappointment was the long queue for breakfast (we ate over the road at the Pavilion in the end) and the smell of smoke in the room from previous guests. The staff did all they could to make our stay comfortable but it is hard to get rid of stale smoke smells.

reviews.title: Personal attention
reviews.text:
I stayed only one night. It was my girl friend's birthday and I booked Ritz Carlton thinking that they are most likely to get something arranged for our late post midnight arrival without needing much explanation. And I was not disappointed!
I called guest relations in the middle of the night before our arrival and basically just asked for a birthday cake to be in the room at arrival the next late evening - and the floor butler outdid himself preparing a really cute arrangemnt for us.
Thanks for that!

Besides that staff was generally very attentive giving the place a really personal feel - not something easy to expect in a large business hotel.
reviews.positives: Central location, attentive staff, good value

reviews.title: Good city center hotel with friendly and pleasant
reviews.text: This is a perfect city center location, close to the heart of the city but still quiet. The staff are genuinely friendly and really nothing is too much for them. My only complaint with this almost perfect hotel is that the lobby lounge, the only place to have a cup of tea has a smoking policy! I had to leave before finishing my afternoon tea as some unthoughful person was smoking a cigar!!

reviews.title: Great service and great room
reviews.text:
The Ritz Carlton KL is located in the heart of KL, to be precise very near to bukit bintang, which makes it a good place for business or leisure. You can get food easily as well as shopping. 
There is a connecting walkway all the way to Starhill which is so much better. 

The staff was very friendly and warm, and being very helpful. 

The room was great, clean and cosy.
reviews.positives: Good location, one of the best service, nice room

reviews.title: Still a great pick for KL
reviews.text: After our 6th stay at this hotel, it is still our pick for KL due to its great location and very good staff. However, where  before it would have rated a 5 out of 5 for everything, some corner cutting by management has seen it drop some standards expected of a 5 star establishment. But still, great value for money.

Close to BB, good for shopping and base to sightseeing. Don't have a bad comment to say really. Didn't use the pool however it looked nice and what you would expect from a city hotel. Would use this hotel again.
